# GitHub Repository Configuration for Maximum Discoverability

## Repository Description (About field)

The following text must be entered in the GitHub repository About field. This is the single most important SEO field for GitHub search. It appears in search results and is indexed by Google. It must be under 350 characters. Copy and paste this exactly.

Production-ready Redis-compatible distributed in-memory cache with native P2P replication. Every node reads and writes. Zero master-slave config. Drop-in Redis/Valkey/Memcached replacement. No Sentinel, no proxy, no code changes. Written in Go.

## Repository Topics (Labels)

The following topics must be added to the repository using the GitHub Topics feature. GitHub topics are searchable and appear in GitHub Explore. Add every topic in this list. Topics must be lowercase with hyphens, no spaces.

redis
redis-compatible
redis-cluster
redis-replication
redis-alternative
memcached
memcached-alternative
memcached-replication
distributed-cache
in-memory-cache
key-value-store
key-value-database
caching
cache
distributed-systems
golang
go
peer-to-peer
p2p-replication
database
nosql
replication
high-availability
clustering
cluster
valkey
valkey-compatible
resp-protocol
resp2
zero-downtime
horizontal-scaling
cache-server
distributed-database
multi-master
eventually-consistent
real-time-replication
drop-in-replacement
microservices
cloud-native
infrastructure
devops
sre
backend
server
tcp
network
performance
low-latency
sub-millisecond

## Repository Website URL

Set the repository website URL to the GitHub Pages URL once documentation is published, or to the project contact email formatted as a mailto link as a placeholder. If no website exists yet, leave blank rather than adding an irrelevant URL.

## Social Preview Image

Create a social preview image for the repository. This appears when the repository link is shared on Twitter, LinkedIn, Slack, and other platforms. The image should be 1280 by 640 pixels. The text on the image should read: Super-Cache — Distributed In-Memory Cache. Every Node Reads and Writes. Drop-in Redis Compatible. No Sentinel. No Proxy. No Code Changes. Use a dark background with white text and a visual element suggesting a mesh network of connected nodes. This image significantly increases click-through rate when the repository is shared.

## Repository Settings Checklist

- [ ] Enable Issues for bug reports and feature requests.
- [ ] Enable Discussions for community questions.
- [ ] Enable the Wiki and create a Home page that mirrors the README introduction.
- [ ] Set the default branch to main.
- [ ] Add branch protection to main requiring at least one review.
- [ ] Enable Dependabot for Go dependency security alerts.
- [ ] Pin the repository if it is in an organisation account.
